data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 AM ET: Global Markets Lower on Concerns Surrounding U.S. Debt | Why ...
data.description:
Global Markets Lower on Concerns Surrounding U.S. Debt

European markets followed Asia lower Thursday, weighed down by concerns over the rising cost of U.S. debt.

----

Why it Isn't Time to Panic About the 20-Year Treasury Auction Yet

What happened on Wednesday may not have been the "breakout" event some investors were looking for.

----

Eurozone Business Activity Declines Despite Tariff Respite

Business activity fell in May even as Trump rolled back some tariff hikes, according to surveys released Thursday.

----

The Deficit Is Unsettling Bond Traders. Here's How That Affects the Economy.

The trade war has calmed down, but rising government borrowing costs pose a new worry.

----

U.S. Trade Deals With Allies â€˜Suboptimal' Solution to Tariff Drag, EU's Dombrovskis Says

A string of U.S.-led deals with key trading partners could help reduce heightened uncertainty in the business community and financial markets but represent a "suboptimal" solution, says the European Union's top economic official.

----

The Fortress That China Built for Its Battle with America

Beijing is racing ahead in advanced technology, including in robots, satellites and AI-and in some cases is catching up with the U.S.

----

Trump Tells European Leaders in Private That Putin Isn't Ready to End War

The president made the acknowledgment after speaking to the Russian leader this week but backed off on additional sanctions.

----

The surprising factor that's driving up home-foreclosure rates

One of the leading drivers of foreclosures in the U.S. is extreme weather, First Street says.

----

DOGE Cuts Are Creeping Into the Labor Market. There Are Telltale Signs.

Job postings data show the cuts are starting to affect the number of openings in research and related fields.

----

GOP Leaders Make Last-Minute Changes to Trump Tax Bill

The changes are intended to placate the party's warring wings just enough to get the bill passed early Thursday.

----

Trump Teases Possibility of Taking Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac Public

Talks about ending government control of the mortgage giants have been under way since at least last year.

----

U.S., Canada Senior Officials Laud Productive Talks on the Sidelines of G-7

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Canadian Finance Minister Francois-Philippe Champagne met on the sidelines of the G-7 as the two countries try to tamp down trade tensions that have roiled the bilateral relationship for months.

----

U.K. Consumer Sentiment Lifts From Post-Trump-Tariff Lows, BRC Says

Consumer confidence in the U.K. picked up in May as the outlook for trade improved and data showed the economy grew more than expected at the start of the year, according to a monthly survey.

----

Mary Daly and Other Fed Presidents Warn Don't Expect Recession or Rate Cuts

The three presidents all emphasized the limits of backward-looking data and the growing importance of anecdotal intelligence.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 5 AM ET: Rio Tinto CEO Jakob Stausholm to Step Down | Honeywell ...
data.description:
Rio Tinto CEO Jakob Stausholm to Step Down

The world's second-biggest miner said Stausholm would continue to lead the company while the process to replace him continues.

----

Honeywell to Buy Johnson Matthey's Catalyst Unit For $2.4 Billion

The U.S. industrials conglomerate agreed to buy Johnson Matthey's catalysts unit, lifting shares in the British platinum refiner.

----

Generali's Profit Beats Views on Property-and-Casualty Strength

The Italian insurer reported better-than-expected profit for the first quarter, driven by the performance of its property-and-casualty division.

----

Lenovo's Profit Falls but Revenue Beats as Tariff Risk Looms

Lenovo Group's quarterly profit dropped first time in more than a year, but revenue beat expectations, capping solid annual results for the world's largest personal-computer maker as it braces for tariff headwinds.

----

What Sam Altman Told OpenAI About the Secret Device He's Making With Jony Ive

The idea is a "chance to do the biggest thing we've ever done as a company here," Altman told OpenAI employees Wednesday.

----

Sanofi to Buy Vigil Neuroscience for About $470 Million

Sanofi has entered an agreement to acquire Vigil Neuroscience for approximately $470 million, adding a new investigational treatment for Alzheimer's disease to the French pharmaceutical company's pipeline.

----

Publicis Deepens Influencer Marketing Push With Deal for Captiv8

The acquisition is said to be valued at $150 million.

----

Tesla Stock Is Rising. Why Investors Should Watch a Key Senate Vote Thursday.

A controversial senate vote on Thursday could impact Tesla's ability to generate hundreds of millions of dollars per quarter by selling zero emission vehicle credits.

----

EV Owners Face Annual Fee Under Proposed Tax Bill

The latest version of President Donald Trump's tax and spending bill includes a provision for an annual fee for EVs and hybrids.

----

Walmart to Cut 1,500 Corporate Jobs in Restructuring

The retailer is planning layoffs in a reorganization aimed at trimming its expenses and speeding up decision-making.

----

Nike to Sell Products on Amazon Following Five-Year Absence

A spokesperson for Amazon said the move will expand its selection for U.S. consumers beyond Nike inventory offered by independent sellers in the last several years.

----

Snowflake's stock has been on fire, and earnings are fueling the rally further

The data-warehousing company hit a revenue milestone and issued an upbeat outlook.

----

Urban Outfitters Sales, Earnings Jump on Strength Across Retail Banners

Urban Outfitters posted double-digit sales growth in the first quarter with revenue growing at each of its main brands, an upbeat sign as other retailers said macroeconomic uncertainty was dampening demand.

----

NBCUniversal Bids for MLB Rights That Were Dropped by ESPN

The company aims to air games on NBC and the Peacock streaming service.

----

AT&T to Buy Lumen's Fiber Business for $5.75 Billion

The business includes one million fiber customers and reaches across 11 states.

FORM 8.3

data.title: Poland checking for any explosive devices near undersea power cable, PM says
data.description:
Poland is looking into whether any explosive devices were planted where a ship from Russia's "shadow fleet" was seen moving suspiciously near a power cable linking Poland with Sweden on Wednesday, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk said.

NATO has beefed up security in the Baltic following a string of incidents in which power cables, telecom links and gas pipelines were damaged in the wake of Russia's invasion of Ukraine in February 2022.

"Shadow fleet" refers to vessels used by Russia to ship oil, arms and grains in violation of international sanctions imposed over its war in Ukraine.

"We are still investigating whether any explosives were planted - it has to be checked very carefully. ORP Heweliusz (Polish hydrographic ship) is still at sea (doing so), and for now there are no worrisome signals," Tusk told reporters on Thursday during a meeting with Polish Navy commanders.

"I am really very satisfied that in a very effective, discreet way, without using unnecessary means, we managed to discourage the ship from any kinetic actions that could cause damage to the power cable," he said.

Defence Minister Wladyslaw Kosiniak-Kamysz said on Wednesday a Polish patrol flight had scared the Russian ship off and the navy's ORP Heweliusz sailed to the scene.

The 600-megawatt undersea cable links the Swedish coast near Karlshamn with Ustka in northern Poland and allows both grids to rely on cross-border supplies when electricity is cheaper in the other system.

On Wednesday Russia's embassy in Warsaw declined to comment. In the past, Moscow has denied involvement in undersea sabotage in the Baltic, saying the West was using such claims to curb Russia's seaborne oil exports.

data.title: S P Setia's Profit Slides in Q1
data.description:
S P Setia said profit attributable to owners dropped to 67 million ringgit in the first quarter from 77.3 million ringgit a year earlier.

Earnings per share rose to 0.0050 ringgit from 0.0047 ringgit a year ago, according to a Wednesday Malaysian bourse filing.

Revenue fell to 770.7 million ringgit versus 1.48 billion ringgit a year prior, the Malaysian infrastructure company said.

data.title: Lost Money on BigBear.ai Holdings, Inc. (BBAI)? Urged to Join Class Action Before June 10, 2025 - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 22, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your BigBear.ai Holdings, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/bigbear-ai-holdings-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=149701&wire=1&utm_campaign=22

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=1030791&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2fNUC8at0gqps" width="0"/></div></div></div><p><strong>THE LAWSUIT:</strong> A class action securities lawsuit was filed against BigBear.ai Holdings, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between March 31, 2022 and March 25, 2025.</p><p><strong>CASE DETAILS:</strong> The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) BigBear maintained deficient accounting review policies related to the reporting and disclosure of certain non-routine, unusual, or complex transactions; (ii) as a result, the Company incorrectly determined that the conversion option within the 2026 Convertible Notes qualified for the derivative scope exception under ASC 815-40 and failed to bifurcate the conversion option as required by ASC 815-15; (iii) accordingly, BigBear had improperly accounted for the 2026 Convertible Notes; (iv) the foregoing error caused BigBear to misstate various items in several of the Company's previously issued financial statements; (v) as a result, these financial statements were inaccurate and would likely need to be restated; (vi) BigBear would require extra time and expense to correct the inaccurate financial statements, thereby increasing the risk that the Company would be unable to timely file certain financial reports with the SEC; and (vii) as a result, the Company's public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.</p><p><strong>WHAT'S NEXT?</strong> If you suffered a loss in BigBear.ai Holdings stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to <a href="https://pr.report/aonv" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/pslra-1/bigbear-ai-holdings-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=149701&wire=1&utm_campaign=22</a> to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. <strong>There is no cost or obligation to participate.</strong></p><p><strong>WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY:</strong> Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.</p><p><strong>CONTACT:</strong><br/>Levi & Korsinsky, LLP<br/>Joseph E. Levi, Esq.<br/>Ed Korsinsky, Esq.<br/>33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor<br/>New York, NY 10004<br/><a href="mailto:jlevi@levikorsinsky.com" rel="nofollow">jlevi@levikorsinsky.com</a><br/>Tel: (212) 363-7500<br/>Fax: (212) 363-7171<br/><a href="https://pr.report/aonw" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/</a></p><p><strong>SOURCE:</strong> Levi & Korsinsky, LLP</p><br/><br/>View the original <a href="https://www.accessnewswire.com/newsroom/en/business-and-professional-services/lost-money-on-bigbear.ai-holdings-inc.-bbai-urged-to-join-class-1030791">press release</a> on ACCESS Newswire<br/><br/><img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/img.ashx?id=1030791" width="0"/></body></html>

data.title: Serbia's NIS plans lower dividend for 2024 amid profit drop
data.description:
BELGRADE (Serbia), May 22 (SeeNews) - Serbia's oil and gas group NIS [BEL:NIIS] said on Wednesday it plans pay a gross dividend of 28.18 dinars ($0.27/ 0.24 euro) per share this year, down from 64.26 dinars per share it distributed last year.

The company plans to distribute a total of 4.6 billion dinars in dividends, a quarter of its last year's net profit of 18.4 billion dinars, NIS' board said on Wednesday in a proposal which shareholders are set to vote on at a meeting scheduled for June 20.

The remaining 13.8 billion dinars from the 2024 net profit will be retained, NIS said. Adding this amount to the undistributed profit from previous years will bring the total undistributed profit of NIS to 300 billion dinars.

NIS’ dividend policy sets a minimum payout of 15% of net profit to shareholders. Since 2009, when Russia’s Gazprom Neft acquired a 51% stake from the Serbian government, the company has distributed 25% of its net profit as dividends each year - except in 2009, 2010, 2011, and 2020, when no dividends were paid.

NIS’ 2024 profit, on which the dividend is based, does not include results from petrochemicals producer HIP Petrohemija, its Q4 financial statement shows. In June 2023, NIS raised its stake in HIP Petrohemija to 90% under a strategic partnership with the Serbian government, which previously held 75.27%.

On January 10, the US Treasury announced sanctions targeting NIS as part of broader measures against Russia's energy sector, citing the control of the company by Russian oil giant Gazprom Neft. The enforcement of the restrictions, which were initially set to take effect on February 27, had been delayed three times: until March 28, until April 28, and most recently until June 27.

When first announcing sanctions against NIS in January, the U.S. Treasury cited Gazprom Neft's control over NIS. At that time, Gazprom Neft held a 50% stake in NIS, while its parent Gazprom had a 6.15% stake. Subsequently, Gazprom Neft transferred a 5.15% stake in NIS to Gazprom, which was not targeted by the sanctions.

Serbia's government is the second largest shareholder in NIS with 29.87%.

On January 14, the Belgrade bourse halted trading in NIS shares due to the expected impact of the sanctions on the company's share price and trading conditions.

data.title: China plans regulation to aid tech firms listing overseas, official says
data.description:
China will provide a more efficient, transparent and predictable regulatory environment to support technology firms listing overseas, Chief Risk Officer at the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) Yan Bojin said at a news briefing on Thursday.

The regulator will further strengthen the security of funds raised by listed companies to ensure their allocation towards the main business rather than other purposes, Yan said.

China has accelerated efforts towards technology self-sufficiency as Washington steps up curbs against Chinese tech advancement, at a time of rising geopolitical tensions between China and the U.S.

The regulator will make greater efforts to support high-quality, unprofitable technology firms going public, said Yan.

The CSRC will also deepen reforms on Shanghai's tech-focused STAR market and Shenzhen's ChiNext board, and will encourage high quality red-chip firms from the technology sector to list their shares domestically.

Firms with mainland Chinese ownership ties that are listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange are colloquially known as red chips.

data.title: AlphaValue/Baader Europe Adjusts Bekaert Estimates
data.description:
AlphaValue/Baader Europe on Wednesday revised its earnings estimates for Bekaert after the Belgian steel wires company's first quarter update.

"Following Bekaert's Q1 update, we have adopted a slightly more cautious view on the group's expected performance in FY25," the research firm said. "All the divisions are likely to face a challenging year as softer industry conditions persist - extending the trend observed in H2 2024," with analysts also noting "lingering uncertainty" from trade and tariffs.

Analysts' EPS forecast for 2025 decreased to 4.76 euros from 5.08 euros, while the 2026 EPS estimate slightly increased to 5.27 euros from 5.23 euros. Full-year 2025 sales are now expected to be flat year over year as the firm raised its estimate by 3%.

The stock is rated at reduce, with a price target of 38.4 euros.

data.title: Suspected Payment Fraud Hits Software Circle's Operating Unit
data.description:
Software Circle said one of its operating units inadvertently sent 426,770 pounds sterling as a result of a suspected authorized push payment fraud.

As a result, the British software-focused investor said Thursday it is conducting a full internal review, and that the incident is also under investigation by a specialist team within the bank.

Software Circle assured there is no evidence of a wider breach of its systems or data.

data.title: CGS International Adjusts Frencken Group's Price Target to SG$1.27 from SG$1.15, Keeps at Add
Crude Oil Prices Drop as OPEC+ Mulls 3rd Straight Month of Output Hike
data.description:
Crude oil prices declined on Thursday on reports that the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and allied producers are mulling a 3rd straight monthly output increase.

Brent crude at last look lost 1.2% to US$64.16/barrel and West Texas Intermediate crude fell 1.1% to US$60.88/b.

OPEC is set to meet on June 1 and is said to be discussing an output hike of 411,000 barrels per day for July, triple the amount initially planned, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing unnamed delegates.

While the news sparked concerns that increasing global supply would exceed demand growth, analysts have been anticipating the move and the 411,000-b/d amount is the "most likely outcome" from the meeting, Reuters quoted RBC Capital analyst Helima Croft as saying.

The U.S. Energy Information Administration also reported surprise increases in crude and fuel stockpiles, with crude inventories rising by 1.3 million barrels, compared with a 1.3-million-barrel drop forecasted by analysts in a Reuters poll.

"The EIA's reported surprise stock builds will have a downward pressure particularly on WTI," Reuters quoted Emril Jamil, a senior analyst at LSEG Oil Research, as saying.

data.title: FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI Index Ends 1.2% Lower at 1527.02
TotalEnergies starts 263-megawatt solar project in Spain
data.description:
Key points:
  • Cluster of 5 solar parks near Seville in Spain
  • Will produce 515 Gigawatt-hours of electricity annually

French oil major TotalEnergies started up a cluster of five solar projects in Seville, Spain, in what will be the company's largest solar project in Europe, it said on Thursday.

The sites have a total capacity of 263 megawatts, and will produce 515 gigawatt-hours of renewable electricity annually — enough to power 150,000 households.
